segments.text: Job went on speaking.
I wish I was back in the old days when God looked after me!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: His light shone above me and lit my way through the darkness.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When I was young and strong, God was my friend and spoke to me in my home.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The Almighty was still with me and I was surrounded with my children.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My herds produced much milk,  and oil flowed freely from my olive presses.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I went out to the city gate and took my seat in the public square.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The young men saw me and moved out of the way; the elders would st and up for me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The leaders remained silent and covered their mouths with their hands.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The voices of the officials were hushed; they held their tongues in my presence.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Everyone who listened to me praised me; whoever saw me commended me,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: because I gave to the poor who called out to me and the orphans who had no one to help them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Those who were about to die blessed me; I made the widow sing for joy.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Being true and acting right were what I wore for clothing.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I was like eyes for the blind and feet for the lame.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I was like a father to the poor, and I defended the rights of strangers.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I broke the jaw of the wicked and made them drop their prey from their teeth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I thought I would die at home, after many years.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Like a tree my roots spread out to the water; the dew rests on my branches overnight.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Fresh honors were always being given to me; my strength was renewed like an unfailing bow.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: People listened carefully to what I had to say; they kept quiet as they listened to my advice.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Once I had spoken they had nothing else to say; what I said was enough.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They waited for me like people waiting for rain; their mouths wide open for the spring rain.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When I smiled at them they could hardly believe it; my approval meant all the world to them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I decided the way forward as their leader, living like a king among his soldiers, and when they were sad I comforted them.
segments.speaker: Unknown
